allowedShipGuardActions: Improve ShipGuard report fields, Markdown, rules, docs, plugin guidance, or public fixtures.
Use private app evidence only to choose the shape of synthetic public eval coverage.
forbiddenActions: Do not edit the scanned app from this report.
Do not open target-app redesign, icon, haptic, motion, or localization tasks from this report.

coherenceRisks.evidence: Synthetic design inventory has visual, motion, haptic, and copy signals but no app-work authorization.
coherenceRisks.title: Design coherence finding must not become target-app work

proofBoundary.expectedArtifact: ios-report-quality.json plus fixture coverage for design coherence boundaries.
proofBoundary.localProof: Run shipguard ios report-quality on this synthetic design fixture.
proofBoundary.manualProof: A human may later authorize target-app design work, but this fixture does not authorize it.

designCoherenceBoundary.purpose: Keep design-system coherence findings as ShipGuard product-QA evidence until target-app work is separately authorized.

shipguardNextAction.expectedArtifact: A public synthetic report-quality fixture that checks the coherence boundary without private app data.
shipguardNextAction.failureMeaning: Private app design evidence can still become unreviewed app remediation advice instead of ShipGuard product QA.
shipguardNextAction.sourceQuestion: Did it separate design-system coherence findings from target-app implementation work?
shipguardNextAction.successCondition: Report-quality fails if a design report turns coherence inventory into target-app implementation work or hides the authorization boundary.

copyTone.stance: specific to the app task and audience

motion.reason: Motion should clarify learning state, feedback, and recovery rather than apply utility-only restraint.

nextAction.expectedArtifact: A same-flow screenshot or preview receipt plus one note mapping the learning-progress profile to source signals.
nextAction.failureMeaning: The design report remains an inventory, not an app-type-specific design QA recommendation.
nextAction.manualProof: Review one synthetic learning flow and confirm motion, haptics, visual density, and copy guidance match the education profile rather than a universal design checklist.
nextAction.successCondition: The report explains why learning-progress is the right profile for education and avoids utility-only advice.

designTailoring.risk: Generic utility restraint can make learning feedback feel flat, while generic game delight can distract from comprehension.

findings.evidence: Synthetic design inventory has visual, motion, haptic, and copy signals but no app-work authorization.
findings.proof: Review the Design Tailoring Contract and Design Coherence Boundary, then run report-quality on the synthetic fixture.
findings.proofGuidance: Review the Design Tailoring Contract and Design Coherence Boundary, then run report-quality on the synthetic fixture.
findings.recommendation: Improve ShipGuard report-quality rules or public fixtures before using this as target-app implementation guidance.
findings.title: Design coherence finding must not become target-app work
